Explanation: Using `context.fetchOne(COMPANY, ...)` fetches all columns from the COMPANY table into a CompanyRecord, even though only the NAME column is used. Consider using `context.select(COMPANY.NAME).from(COMPANY).where(COMPANY.NAME.eq(name)).fetchOne()` to explicitly select only the required columns.

Using `context.fetch(PRODUCT)` fetches all columns from the PRODUCT table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using `context.select(...).from(PRODUCT).fetch()`.

Using `selectFrom(PRODUCT)` fetches all columns from the PRODUCT table, which may include unnecessary data. Consider using `select(PRODUCT.ID, PRODUCT.NAME, PRODUCT.COMPANY_NAME, PRODUCT.AMOUNT).from(PRODUCT)` to explicitly specify only the needed columns.
